audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for sending the Twelve, Herod's question, feeding five thousand, Peter confession, passion prediction, cross-following, transfiguration, difficult healing, greatness, outsider in the name, and setting face toward Jerusalem.

verses.restored: Calling together the twelve, he gave them power and authority over all unclean spirits and to heal diseases.
He sent them to proclaim the reign of the Divine and to heal.
He said to them, Take nothing for the way: neither staff, nor bag, nor bread, nor silver, nor two tunics each.
Whatever house you enter, remain there, and from there go out.
As many as do not receive you, going out from that city, shake off the dust from your feet as a testimony against them.
Going out, they traveled through the villages, announcing good news and healing everywhere.
Herod the tetrarch heard all that was happening and was perplexed, because some were saying John had been raised from the dead.
Others said Elijah had appeared, and others that one of the ancient prophets had risen.
Herod said, John I beheaded; but who is this about whom I hear such things? And he sought to see him.
When the apostles returned, they described to him all they had done. Taking them, he withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
But the crowds, knowing, followed him. Receiving them, he spoke to them about the reign of the Divine and healed those needing healing.

The day began to decline. The twelve came and said to him, Release the crowd, so they may go into the surrounding villages and farms to lodge and find provisions, because we are here in a deserted place.
He said to them, You give them something to eat. They said, We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we go and buy food for all these people.
For there were about five thousand men. He said to his disciples, Make them recline in groups of about fifty each.
They did so and made them all recline.
Taking the five loaves and two fish, looking up to heaven, he blessed them, broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
All ate and were filled, and what remained was taken up: twelve baskets of broken pieces.
It happened, as he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he questioned them, saying, Who do the crowds say I am?
They answered, John the Baptist; others, Elijah; others, that one of the ancient prophets has risen.
He said to them, But you, who do you say I am? Peter answered, The Messiah of the Divine.
He strictly warned and instructed them to tell this to no one,
saying, It is necessary for the Son of Humanity to suffer many things, be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, be killed, and on the third day be raised.
He said to all, If anyone wants to come after me, let that one deny self, take up one's cross daily, and follow me.
For whoever wants to save one's life/soul will lose it, but whoever loses one's life/soul because of me, this one will save it.
For what does a person profit, having gained the whole world, but losing or forfeiting oneself?
Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, of this one the Son of Humanity will be ashamed when he comes in his glory and the glory of the Father and of the holy messengers.
Truly I say to you: there are some standing here who will certainly not taste death until they see the reign of the Divine.
About eight days after these words, he took Peter, John, and James and went up into the mountain to pray.
As he was praying, the appearance of his face became different, and his clothing became white, flashing like lightning.
Look, two men were speaking with him: Moses and Elijah,
who appeared in glory and spoke of his exodus/departure, which he was about to fulfill in Jerusalem.
Peter and those with him had been weighed down with sleep; but becoming fully awake, they saw his glory and the two men standing with him.

As they were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, Master, it is beautiful/good for us to be here; let us make three tents, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah, not knowing what he was saying.
As he was saying these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them; they feared as they entered the cloud.
A voice came from the cloud, saying, This is my Son, the Chosen One; listen to him.
When the voice had happened, Jesus was found alone. They kept silent and reported to no one in those days anything of what they had seen.
On the next day, when they came down from the mountain, a great crowd met him.
Look, a man from the crowd cried out, Teacher, I beg you to look upon my son, because he is my only child.
Look, a spirit seizes him, and suddenly he cries out; it convulses him with foam and scarcely departs from him, bruising him.
I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they were not able.
Jesus answered, O untrusting and twisted generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.
As he was coming, the troubling spirit threw him down and convulsed him. Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
All were struck with wonder at the majesty of the Divine. While all were marveling at all he was doing, he said to his disciples,
Place these words in your ears: the Son of Humanity is about to be handed over into human hands.
But they did not understand this word; it was hidden from them so they would not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this word.
A reasoning entered among them about which of them might be greatest.
Jesus, knowing the reasoning of their heart, took a child and stood the child beside him,
and said to them, Whoever receives this child in my name receives me, and whoever receives me receives the One who sent me; for the smaller one among all of you, this one is great.
John answered, Master, we saw someone casting out unclean spirits in your name, and we tried to stop him because he does not follow with us.
Jesus said to him, Do not stop him, for whoever is not against you is for you.
It happened, when the days were being fulfilled for his taking up, he set his face to go to Jerusalem.
He sent messengers before his face. Going, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for him.
They did not receive him, because his face was going toward Jerusalem.
Seeing this, the disciples James and John said, Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from heaven and consume them?
But turning, he rebuked them.
And they went to another village.
As they were going on the way, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
Jesus said to him, Foxes have holes and birds of heaven nests, but the Son of Humanity has nowhere to lay the head.
He said to another, Follow me. But he said, Lord, permit me first to go away and bury my father.
He said to him, Leave the dead to bury their own dead; but you, going away, proclaim the reign of the Divine.
Another also said, I will follow you, Lord, but first permit me to say farewell to those in my house.
Jesus said to him, No one putting hand to plow and looking back is fit for the reign of the Divine.
